commit.message:
fix(tests): update evening review timeout mocks

Summary:
- update the evening-review timeout test to use the new helper tuple contract
- keep the timeout branch exercising the real LLM timeout path instead of failing early on unpack

Rationale:
- preserve coverage for the timeout behavior after the degraded-input helper changes

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/tests/test_timeouts.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_timeouts.py tests/test_evening_review.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(audit): trace evening review degradation

Summary:
- log degraded evening-review input fallbacks instead of treating partial data failures as clean success
- add deterministic eval and backend regression coverage for the degraded-input audit contract
- update implementation and testing docs so the shipped runtime reliability surface stays current

Rationale:
- make partial helper failures visible in scheduler audit data without changing user-facing fallback behavior
- keep the evening-review boundary aligned with the rest of the runtime reliability hardening work

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/scheduler/jobs/evening_review.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_evening_review.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_evening_review.py tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario evening_review_degraded_inputs_audit --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Allow overriding bibliography style (#359)

Add scaffold.bibstyle option and pass it through to the
generated GAPDoc bibliography element so package manuals can
choose a LaTeX bibliography style such as alphaurl.

This commit was prepared with Codex, which assisted with
implementation, testing, and documentation updates.

Resolves #352

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs(learn): place learnings in appropriate sections

- fall back to creating a fitting section and use Codex Learnings only as a last resort
- require each inserted bullet to end with (Codex learning)

Rationale:

- the suffix preserves provenance without forcing a dedicated heading for every entry

commit.message:
docs(tool-use-symphony-setup): switch to runtime upstream proxy

Summary:
- Rewrote tool-use-symphony-setup as a thin proxy skill.
- Removed local duplicated setup references and workflow template copies.
- Added runtime fetch instructions pointing to Symphony upstream skill.

Rationale:
- Prevent setup drift between central skill docs and the tool's source of
  truth.
- Ensure latest setup flow is always used by fetching upstream at runtime.
- Avoid silent fallback to stale local copies when upstream changes.

Tests:
- not run (documentation-only change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: iOS pod install 자동 감지 기준 수정 (#36)

* fix: iOS pod install 자동 판정으로 전환

- iOS 빌드에서 pod install 필요 여부를 워크스페이스 상태와 SDK 변경 기준으로 자동 판단합니다.
- spec 해석 문제일 때만 --repo-update 를 재시도하도록 조정했습니다.
- 관련 환경 조립과 테스트를 함께 보강했습니다.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: iOS pod install 자동 감지 기준 수정

- Manifest.lock이 없는 워크스페이스에서도 pod 상태 파일을 유연하게 판단하도록 수정
- 실제 CocoaPods 산출물인 Pods.xcodeproj 기준으로 pod install 필요 여부를 확인
- 검증: bash -n action/1_ios.sh, make doctor

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- normalize GitHub origin URLs to SSH before exporting the Claworld
  push remote into Symphony runs
- update the launcher README examples to show the canonical SSH push
  remote instead of the HTTPS URL

Rationale:

  the Claworld workflow now treats as non-canonical
- keeping the launcher and project workflow aligned makes publish-time
  behavior deterministic across agent workspaces

Summary:
- retarget the parallel refill queue to the next broker validation and
  rollback-policy slices
- mark the four recycled Linear tickets as the current active batch in the
  queue manifests

  set of runnable parallel-safe slices
- keeping the queue manifests aligned with Linear lets Symphony refill the next
  work without manual reshuffling

commit.message:
feat(claworld): bootstrap from a canonical local mirror

Summary:
- resolve the canonical Claworld source repo and source ref in the
  dedicated launcher
- require workflow-scoped Linear env vars before starting Symphony
- document the launcher-only Claworld workflow mode and override envs

Rationale:
- keep remote sync at the launcher boundary instead of relying on
  implicit clone or pull behavior inside issue workspaces
- make the project-owned Claworld harness easier to start correctly
  and easier to reason about

Tests:
- bash -n scripts/option2-run.sh scripts/run-claworld.sh
- source ~/.zshrc >/dev/null 2>&1; ./scripts/run-claworld.sh
  (observed HAC-111 spawn and transition to In Progress)

commit.message:
fix: 로그인 키체인 unlock 실패를 허용 (#31)

* fix: 대시보드 스텝별 로그 아코디언 추가

- 상세보기 모달에서 각 스테이지를 아코디언으로 렌더링\n- 빌드 로그에 스테이지 메타데이터를 저장하고 응답에 포함\n- 진행 중이거나 실패한 스텝은 기본으로 펼쳐지도록 조정\n\nCo-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: HTTPS 저장소 인증 실패 처리 수정

- HTTPS REPO_URL에서도 빌드 전용 자격증명 설정을 강제합니다.
- macOS keychain helper 대신 workspace .gitconfig와 .git-credentials를 우선 사용합니다.
- 비대화형 clone에서 프롬프트와 keychain 조회를 막습니다.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: 빌드 워크스페이스 슬롯 재사용 구조로 정리

- Shorebird 안드로이드 패치 전 아키텍처 사전 점검을 추가합니다.
- SetupExecutor 책임을 pub, ruby, platform 준비 단계로 분리합니다.
- 공유 slot 기반 워크스페이스 풀을 도입해 같은 브랜치 빌드를 병렬 처리할 수 있게 합니다.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: iOS 키체인 및 Bundler 환경 준비 보강

- iOS toolchain 준비 단계에서 keychain unlock 및 기본 keychain 설정 추가
- Ruby 버전별 GEM_HOME/BUNDLE_PATH 분리로 Bundler gem set 충돌 완화
- 관련 테스트와 빌드 스크립트 환경 변수 검증 보강

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: 로그인 키체인 unlock 실패를 허용

- login.keychain unlock 실패를 경고로만 처리하고 기존 세션 상태로 계속 진행
- custom keychain은 기존처럼 unlock 실패 시 즉시 실패 유지
- 로그인 키체인 fallback 동작을 검증하는 테스트 추가

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(settings): split account controls from app settings

Summary:
- move personal settings into new /account and /account/team pages
- remove user-management destinations from functional navigation
- simplify the session dropdown to user settings and sign out only

Rationale:
- separate operational costing and billing flows from user-specific
  preferences and access management
- give invites and password management a dedicated team-access page
  without exposing user settings in the top app header

Tests:
- npm run typecheck
- git diff --check
- runtime validation via npm run dev with mocked session/user APIs
  for /settings, /account, /account/team, and /settings/users
- npm run lint not run (next lint prompts for initial ESLint setup)

commit.message:
feat(audit): trace screen repository runtime paths

Summary:
- add runtime audit logging to screen observation daily summary, weekly summary, and cleanup repository boundaries
- extend the deterministic runtime eval harness and repository tests to cover screen summary and cleanup audit outcomes
- update implementation status and testing docs so Runtime Reliability reflects the shipped screen-repository boundary

Rationale:
- make observer aggregation and cleanup behavior visible at the storage boundary instead of only through coarse scheduler job logs
- keep the source-of-truth docs aligned with the actual repo state on develop

Tests:
- PYTHONPYCACHEPREFIX=/tmp/pycache python3 -m py_compile backend/src/observer/screen_repository.py backend/src/evals/harness.py backend/tests/test_screen_observation.py backend/tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest tests/test_screen_observation.py tests/test_eval_harness.py
- cd backend && UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run python -m src.evals.harness --scenario screen_repository_runtime_audit --indent 2
- cd backend && OPENROUTER_API_KEY=test-key WORKSPACE_DIR=/tmp/seraph-test UV_CACHE_DIR=/tmp/uv-cache uv run pytest -v
- cd docs && npm run build
- git diff --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
0.9.10 (#463) * fix: strip image blocks from release notes on website The release notes contain a mascot image that renders as a broken or unwanted image on the website. Strip HTML

blocks from release body before rendering. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: derive project root from state_file path in do_import_run follow-up scan When running `desloppify review --import-run --scan-after-import`, the follow-up scan was using _runtime_project_root() which could return a contaminated path (pointing to the results directory instead of the actual project root). This caused state to be written to the wrong location. Instead, derive the project root from the state_file parameter which is known to be correct: state_file.parent.parent gives us the project root from `/.desloppify/state-.json`. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: preserve plan_start_scores during force-rescan to protect manual clusters _reset_cycle_for_force_rescan() was clearing plan_start_scores, which made is_mid_cycle() return False. This caused auto_cluster_issues() to run full cluster regeneration instead of early-returning, wiping manual cluster items via issue ID reconciliation in scan_issue_reconcile.py. The fix stops clearing plan_start_scores so is_mid_cycle() remains True during force-rescan, preserving manual cluster data. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * feat: require explicit triage decisions for auto-clusters Auto-clusters (auto/unused, auto/security, etc.) were silently left in backlog because the triage prompt said "silence means leave in backlog" and the output schema had no field for auto-cluster decisions. Now the triager must make an explicit promote/skip/break_up decision for each auto-cluster, and apply_triage_to_plan() processes those decisions. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * feat: require explicit backlog decisions for auto-clusters in staged triage The staged triage pipeline previously treated auto-clusters as optional in the reflect stage ("silence means it stays in backlog"). This change makes auto-cluster decisions mandatory, matching the treatment review issues get via the Coverage Ledger. Changes: - Reflect instructions: require a ## Backlog Decisions section listing every auto-cluster with promote/skip/supersede (replaces "silence means leave") - Organize instructions: clarify that ALL backlog decisions from reflect must be executed, not just promotions - Reflect validation: parse and persist BacklogDecision entries; warn (but don't block) when auto-clusters exist without a Backlog Decisions section - Organize validation: warn when reflect requested promotions that weren't executed during organize Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * feat: unified triage pipeline + step detail display improvements Unified triage pipeline: - Widen is_triage_finding to all defects (mechanical + review + concern) - Sub-group auto-clusters by rule kind (auto/security-B602 instead of auto/security) - Add MEDIUM+LOW bandit filter and skip_tests config option - Auto-cluster statistical summaries in triage prompt (severity, confidence, samples) - Cluster-level observe sampling (ClusterVerdict parsing) - Blocking backlog decisions validation (every auto-cluster must have a decision) - Threshold-based staleness (10% mechanical growth, any new review issue) - Two-tier accounting: review issues get per-item ledger, mechanical via cluster decisions - Auto-add manual cluster members to queue_order on add_to_cluster Display improvements: - cluster show: steps now show effort tag, wrapped detail (4 lines), short refs - cluster show: members compact when steps exist (ID list, not full issue detail) - cluster list --verbose: effort summary column (3T 1S), hide empty auto-clusters, drop noise columns - next: cluster drill header shows step done markers and effort tags - next: individual task shows full untruncated step detail matched via issue_refs - next: focus mode shows cluster context + relevant step detail Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * feat: lifecycle transition messages and agent directives Add transition_messages config and directives CLI for phase-specific agent instructions (model switching, constraints). Emit transition messages at lifecycle phase changes across resolve, skip, reopen, review import, and reconcile flows. Auto-focus cluster during mid-cluster execution so desloppify next stays in context. Hermes reset includes cluster-aware next-task instructions. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * feat: add dev test-hermes command and bump skill doc version desloppify dev test-hermes: smoke-test Hermes model switching by switching to a random model and back. Skill doc version bumped to v6. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: handle missing git in review coordinator, remove unused import Wrap git status call in try/except OSError so review coordinator doesn't crash when git is unavailable. Remove unused triage_scoped_plan import from stage_validation. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * docs: update Hermes overlay for delegate_task, add directives docs, update website Rewrite HERMES.md: delegate_task subagent pattern replaces worktree-based parallel review. Add agent directives section to SKILL.md. Website: initiative #2 now active with $1k bounty challenge details. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix(r): correct shell quote escaping in lintr command (PR #424) Co-Authored-By: Maximilian Scholz * feat(r): add Jarl as fast R linter with autofix (PR #425) Co-Authored-By: Maximilian Scholz * fix: phpstan stderr/JSON parser fixes (PR #420) Co-Authored-By: Nick Perkins * fix(engine): prevent workflow::create-plan re-injection after resolution (PR #435) Co-Authored-By: Charles Dunda * feat: add SCSS language plugin (PR #428) Co-Authored-By: Klaus Agnoletti * fix: Rust dep graph hangs from string-literal fake imports (PR #429) Co-Authored-By: Riccardo Spagni * fix: binding-aware unused import detection for JS/TS (PR #433) Co-Authored-By: Tom * fix: project root detection, force-rescan plan wipe, and manual cluster visibility (PR #439) * perf(scan): detector prefetch + cache for faster scans (PR #432) Co-Authored-By: Tom * feat(frameworks): FrameworkSpec layer + Next.js spec (PR #414) Co-Authored-By: Tom Swift * fix: allow scan when queue is fully drained regardless of lifecycle phase Fixes #441 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: quote paths for Windows cmd /c and use utf-8 encoding in log recovery Fixes #442 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: merge retry batch results with original run before coverage check Fixes #443 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix(docs): SKILL.md cleanup — remove unsupported frontmatter, fix file naming, generalize install Fixes #444, #445, #446 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * cleanup: remove dead _strip_c_style_comments_preserve_lines shim from rust/tools.py Follow-up to PR #440 (Rust inline-test filtering). Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * refactor: move queue_total==0 check into score_display_mode (#441) Move the empty-queue guard from scan_queue_preflight into score_display_mode() so ALL callers (status, plan nudge, next flow) benefit from the fix, not just scan preflight. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: extract anonymous functions in tree-sitter specs (R lang) PR #449 added an R anonymous function query pattern that captures @fn but the extractor requires @name, silently skipping all anonymous function matches. Fix the extractor to synthesize an "" name when @name is absent but @func is present. Original R spec contributed by sims1253 in PR #449. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix(docs): sync .agents SKILL.md with docs copy, add pip fallback and batch naming note - Remove `allowed-tools` frontmatter from .agents/skills/desloppify/SKILL.md (#444) - Add `pip install` fallback note alongside uvx in both copies (#446) - Add batch output naming clarification (batch-N.raw.txt vs .json imports) (#445) - Sync agent directives section and version bump to .agents copy Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: collapse cmd /c arguments into single string for proper Windows quoting The previous fix pre-quoted the executable path, but the actual breakage was in argument paths (-C repo_root, -o output_file) containing spaces. Pre-embedding quotes in a subprocess list causes double-quoting because Popen's list2cmdline() adds its own quotes. The real issue: cmd /c concatenates everything after /c and re-parses it with its own tokeniser. The fix introduces _wrap_cmd_c() which uses subprocess.list2cmdline() to build the inner command as a single properly-quoted string, then passes that as one token after /c: ["cmd", "/c", "codex exec -C \"path with spaces\" ..."]. - Revert incorrect executable pre-quoting in _resolve_executable - Add _wrap_cmd_c() to properly collapse cmd /c commands - Apply _wrap_cmd_c in codex_batch_command after building the full arg list - Keep correct encoding="utf-8", errors="replace" fix in io.py - Add tests for _wrap_cmd_c and Windows codex_batch_command path quoting Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: skip coverage gate on partial batch retry instead of merging results Replace the 195-line merge approach (find_prior_run_merged_results + overlay_retry_results_on_prior) with a ~5-line bypass: when --only-batches selects a subset of the packet's batches, set allow_partial=True so the coverage gate does not reject the partial retry. The merge approach had multiple issues: wrong prior-run selection after failed retry chains, dimension name normalization mismatches, and stale metadata in combined output. The simpler fix recognizes that a partial retry inherently cannot cover all dimensions, and the original run already handled the rest. Fixes #443 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * bump version to 0.9.10 * bump version to 0.9.10 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * bump version to 0.9.10 Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * chore: gitignore .agents/ and untrack generated skill doc The .agents/skills/desloppify/SKILL.md is a generated file (same as .claude/skills/). Canonical copies live under docs/. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix: add hermes and droid to update-skill help text Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * docs: draft 0.9.10 release notes Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * feat(ruby): improve plugin — excludes, detect markers, default_src, spec/ support, README, tests (#462) * feat(ruby): improve plugin — excludes, detect markers, default_src, README, tests Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Co-Authored-By: Gemini Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex * feat(ruby): add spec/ test dir, bin/ exclusion; expose external_test_dirs in generic_lang - Add external_test_dirs and test_file_extensions parameters to generic_lang() so plugins can override the hardcoded ["tests", "test"] defaults - Configure Ruby plugin with external_test_dirs=["spec", "test"] (RSpec + Minitest) - Add bin/ to Ruby exclusions (binstubs/shims) - Update tests: add bin/ to excluded dirs list, add test_external_test_dirs_includes_spec Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Co-Authored-By: Gemini Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex * docs(ruby): add bin/ to exclusions list in README Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Co-Authored-By: Gemini Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 Co-authored-by: Gemini Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex * feat: add Factory Droid skill harness support (#451) - Add 'droid' to SKILL_TARGETS (.factory/skills/desloppify/SKILL.md) - Add .factory/skills/ to SKILL_SEARCH_PATHS for auto-discovery - Create docs/DROID.md overlay with review and triage workflow - Bump SKILL_VERSION to 6 - Add droid to README agent prompt harness list * docs(python): add user-facing section to README (#459) Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * feat(javascript): add plugin tests and documentation (#458) Co-authored-by: Gemini Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 * fix(docs): correct autofix command in Ruby and JS plugin READMEs The command is `desloppify autofix`, not `desloppify fix` or `desloppify scan --fix`. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * docs: update release notes with late-merged PRs and stats Add #458, #459, #462 contributions from klausagnoletti. Update stats to reflect final commit/file/test counts. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) * fix(scss): replace {file_path} placeholders with glob patterns and use unix formatter The tool runner does not substitute {file_path} placeholders, so stylelint was receiving literal "{file_path}" and failing silently. Switch to glob patterns (matching every other plugin) and use --formatter unix with the gnu parser, since stylelint's JSON output doesn't match the expected json parser format. Based on findings from @klausagnoletti in PR #452. Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) --------- Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) Co-authored-by: Maximilian Scholz Co-authored-by: Nick Perkins Co-authored-by: Charles Dunda Co-authored-by: Klaus Agnoletti Co-authored-by: Riccardo Spagni Co-authored-by: Tom Co-authored-by: Klaus Agnoletti <24544601+klausagnoletti@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: Gemini Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex
